name: running-bug-review-board
description: Real-user QA workflow with a Bug Review Board (BRB). Drives the live app like a customer, files structured P0/P1/P2 bug reports, generates a Zite + Dieter Rams-inspired HTML dashboard (magazine typography, ink-on-paper palette, no chips or pills, 640px reading column, mobile thumb-zone), and produces a YES/NO sign-off per phase. v0.3 redesigns the report away from coloured chips toward an editorial information hierarchy: Title -> Deck -> Impact -> Actual / Expected -> Risk to fix -> Steps -> Evidence. New Impact and Risk-to-fix bug fields (additive — old bugs render gracefully). Discovers and confirms your issue tracker (Linear MCP, GitHub Issues via gh, Jira, Notion) before syncing — never assumed silently. Bi-directional sync pulls engineering's status changes back. For iOS / iPadOS app repos, orchestrates QA and defers simulator driving to the iOS community's purpose-built skills (Cameron Cooke's AXe and XcodeBuildMCP, Rudrank Riyam's App Store Connect CLI + skills, Conor Luddy's ios-simulator-skill, Josh Adams's ios-build-verify, tddworks' baguette, and more). Includes a separate Interactive BRB workflow with pattern-based triage suggestions. Repo-agnostic, browser-tool-agnostic.
